perturbation.original_text: Each Receiving Party will make all reasonable efforts to ensure the protection, confidentiality, and security of any Confidential Information of Disclosing Party in its possession, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Receiving Party to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information, but in no event less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiving Party will make efforts, at its sole discretion, to ensure the protection, confidentiality, and security of any Confidential Information of Disclosing Party in its possession. The level of effort is not standardized and may vary depending on internal resources and priorities of the Recieving Party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates 'all reasonable efforts,' setting a relatively high standard of care. The change replaces this with 'efforts, at its sole discretion,' significantly lowering the required level of care and introducing subjectivity. This directly contradicts the obligation initially established and makes enforcement unclear. The contradicton happens in the same part of the contract, as this is the one that should be followed but the modificaiton makes it hard to be enforceable. The contradiction takes place in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party will make efforts, at its sole discretion, to ensure the protection, confidentiality, and security of any Confidential Information of Disclosing Party in its possession. The level of effort is not standardized and may vary depending on internal resources and priorities of the Recieving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'all reasonable efforts' for protecting confidential information, setting a clear standard. The changed text replaces this with 'efforts, at its sole discretion,' allowing the Receiving Party to decide the level of effort, thus potentially offering very little protection. This directly contradicts the standard of care initially established in the original text and makes enforcement uncertain. The Changed Text and Contradicted Text are identical here. The Change, by allowing 'sole discretion' contradicts the original requirement of 'all reasonable efforts'. This creates an in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will not use any Confidential Information of Disclosing Party for any reason other than the Purpose without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party may use Confidential Information of Disclosing Party for any reason that indirectly supports the Purpose, even without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. Direct use outside of the Purpose still requires written consent.
perturbation.explanation: This change adds ambiguity. The original text strictly prohibits use outside the defined 'Purpose' without consent. The modified version allows for 'indirectly supports the Purpose' usage without consent, creating a loophole. What constitutes 'indirectly supports' is not defined, leading to potential disputes and undermining the original restriction. The contradiciton happens here, due to this change. The contradiciton takes place in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Receiving Party may use Confidential Information of Disclosing Party for any reason that indirectly supports the Purpose, even without the prior written consent of Disclosing Party. Direct use outside of the Purpose still requires written consent.
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibits using confidential information outside the defined 'Purpose' without prior written consent. The changed text introduces an exception by allowing 'indirect' use that 'supports' the Purpose, even without consent. This creates ambiguity as 'indirectly supports' is undefined, undermining the original restriction and causing a contradiction within the document, since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text and thus the contradiciton remains. This adheres to the Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction, where a clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: Each Receiving Party’s obligations under the Agreement will survive termination of the Agreement and will continue until the end of the Confidentiality Term.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Receiving Party’s obligations under the Agreement will survive termination of the Agreement for a period of one year, or until the end of the Confidentiality Term, whichever comes first. However, certain obligations, such as the duty to not reverse engineer any products, will not extend beyond the Agreement Term.
perturbation.explanation: The original text clearly states that all obligations survive until the end of the 'Confidentiality Term'. The change introduces a one-year limitation after termination, creating a conflict. Additionally, it adds that the duty to not reverse engineer products won't extend beyond the 'Agreement Term', introducing another conflict with the original survival clause. This introduces a contradiction with what was defined before. The contradiction happens in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Receiving Party’s obligations under the Agreement will survive termination of the Agreement for a period of one year, or until the end of the Confidentiality Term, whichever comes first. However, certain obligations, such as the duty to not reverse engineer any products, will not extend beyond the Agreement Term.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that obligations survive until the end of the 'Confidentiality Term'. The changed text introduces limitations: a one-year limitation and an exclusion for the duty not to reverse engineer products. This directly contradicts the original unlimited survival clause, creating a clear in-text contradiction as it limits the survival of obligations where previously there was no such explicit limit. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which is possible if a text is modified in a way that contradicts itself.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that, in the event of breach or threatened breach or intended breach of the Agreement, each Party, in addition to any other rights and remedies available to it at law or in equity, may seek injunctive or equitable relief without the necessity of posting bond or proving that it has no adequate remedy at law.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that, in the event of a material breach of the Agreement, each Party may seek injunctive or equitable relief, provided that the Party seeking such relief first posts a bond deemed reasonable by the court and demonstrates that monetary damages would be an insufficient remedy.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause allows for injunctive relief without a bond or proof of inadequate remedy at law. The modified clause introduces the requirements of posting a bond and demonstrating inadequate monetary damages. This directly contradicts the original provision, making it more difficult to obtain injunctive relief. Introducing a more restrictive remedy. The contradiction happens in the same section.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that, in the event of a material breach of the Agreement, each Party may seek injunctive or equitable relief, provided that the Party seeking such relief first posts a bond deemed reasonable by the court and demonstrates that monetary damages would be an insufficient remedy.
perturbation.justification: The original text allows for injunctive relief without posting a bond or proving inadequate remedy. The changed text requires a bond and proof of inadequate monetary damages. This directly contradicts the original provision, introducing requirements where none existed before. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the contradicted text contains the changed text, creating a direct contradiction between the original allowance of injunctive relief without conditions and the changed text's imposition of conditions (posting a bond and demonstrating inadequate monetary damages) for seeking such relief.
